1 Introduction  ...    In Paris, the River Seine lies on an alluvial plain whose elevation ranges between 27 and 30 m above sea level (msl) (Figure 1). The entrenchment into the plateau above reaches ca. 100 m. The QuaiBranly site is situated at the apex of the convex left bank of the river meander that flows through Paris. The alluvial plain is made up of overwash deposits developed along an approximately 3-km-wide belt  ...
